questions.solution: $y=\tan x=\frac{1}{x^2}$ From graph it is clear that it will have two real roots,

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\cos 3 x+\sin \left(2 x-\frac{7 \pi}{6}\right)=-2$, then $x$ is equal to $($ where $m \in I)$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 1+\cos 3 x+1-\left[\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}+\left(2 x-\frac{7 \pi}{6}\right)\right)\right]=0 \\ & 2 \cos ^2 \frac{3 x}{2}+1-\cos \left(2 x-\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)=0 \\ & 2 \cos ^2 \frac{3 x}{2}+2 \sin ^2\left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)=0\end{aligned}$
$\cos \frac{3 x}{2}=0, \sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)=0$
$\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{3}, \pi$ and $\mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{3}, \frac{2 \pi}{3}, \frac{7 \pi}{3}, \ldots$
$\therefore \mathrm{x}=\frac{\pi}{3}$ is the common value which satisfies both
$\therefore x=2 n \pi+\frac{\pi}{3}=(6 n+1) \frac{\pi}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\sin x+\sin y \geq \cos \alpha \cos x \forall x \in R$ then $\sin y+\cos \alpha$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { As } \sin x+\sin y \geq \cos \alpha \cos x \forall x \in R \\ & \text { Let } x=-\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow \sin y \geq 1 \Rightarrow \sin y=1 \\ & \Rightarrow 1+\sin x \geq \cos \alpha \cos x \\ & \Rightarrow \cos \alpha \cos x-\sin x \leq 1 \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{\cos ^2 \alpha+1}=1 \Rightarrow \cos \alpha=0 \Rightarrow \sin y+\cos \alpha=1+0=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\tan A, \tan B$ are the roots of the quadratic equation $a b x^2-c^2 x+a b=0$ where $a, b, c$ are sides of the $\triangle A B C$ then $\sin ^2 A+\sin ^2 B+\sin ^2 C$ is
questions.solution:
Here, $\tan \mathrm{A}+\tan \mathrm{B}=\frac{\mathrm{c}^2}{\mathrm{ab}}$ and $\tan \mathrm{A} \cdot \tan \mathrm{B}=1$
$\tan A \cdot \tan B=1 \Rightarrow \tan A=\cot B \Rightarrow A=90^{\circ}-B \Rightarrow A+B=90^{\circ} \Rightarrow C=90^{\circ}$ 
$\Rightarrow \sin A=\frac{a}{c}, \sin B=\frac{b}{c}$
$\therefore \sin ^2 A+\sin ^2 B+\sin ^2 C=\frac{a^2}{c^2}+\frac{b^2}{c^2}+1=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\sin \theta$ and $-\cos \theta$ are the roots of the equation $a x^2-b x-c=0$, where $a, b, c$ are the sides of a triangle $A B C$, then $\cos B$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sin \theta-\cos \theta=\frac{b}{a} \\ & \sin \theta \cos \theta=\frac{c}{a} \\ & \therefore b^2=a^2-2 a c \\ & \therefore \cos B=\frac{a^2+c^2-b^2}{2 a c}=1+\frac{c}{2 a}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
